title: hotato voice trace, schema hotato.voice_trace.v1
description: An observability bridge: a timeline of discrete voice-pipeline events (audio activity, TTS cancel/stop, ASR partials, tool calls, ...) attached alongside a failure contract's frame-level timing evidence. Built by `hotato trace ingest --otel` from an OTel-flavored source and written as JSONL (one meta line then one line per span; see docs/TRACE.md). Additive-only: new span types and new fields may appear without a version bump; a consumer must ignore unknown fields and unknown span types. A voice trace is supporting evidence, not a verdict: Hotato does not prove root cause from a trace, only that a pattern of events coincided with the measured timing.

call_id.description: Redacted (null) by default. Pass --include-identifiers at ingest time to keep it.

agent_id.description: Redacted (null) by default. Pass --include-identifiers at ingest time to keep it.

format.description: How the spans below were parsed from the ingested file: 'otel-json' (a standard OTel resourceSpans export, best-effort span/event flattening) or 'otel-jsonl-bridge' (hotato's own documented per-line span shape; see docs/OTEL.md). Never claims full OTel wire-protocol coverage.

span.description: One discrete voice-pipeline event. type is an open string set; common values: caller_audio_active, agent_audio_active, tts_cancel_requested, tts_audio_stopped, asr_partial (carries text_redacted, and text only when --include-text was passed), tool_call (carries name, latency_ms), http_exchange (a recorded request/response report -- method, url, status_code, response; hotato never performs the request during assertion evaluation), llm_first_token, handoff. Exactly one time shape applies: an interval span carries start_sec/end_sec, a point event carries time_sec.
